section.title: sdbimprt
section.p: sdbimprt is a import tool in SequoiaDB database, it can achieve from a JSON format or a CSV format data stored file import into SequoiaDB database.

The record of JSON format must meet the json definition:Left and right curly braces ({})as a record delimiter; and the string data type must be contained in doublequotation marks; and escape character is defined as the backslash(\).

CSV is called for short Comma-Separated Value, each record is separate by 0x0D in default, and fields separated by commas. Users can specify the delimiter between fields, as well as the records. Delimiters defined(only support ASCII characters):

strow.stentry: return the basic help and usage information.
import data from SequoiaDB of the specified hostname.In default,the sdbexprt try to connect to local hostname.
specify port.In default,the sdbimprt try to connect to the host which port number is 50000.
specify the format of import data.It can be CSV or JSON.
specify the name of file want to import.
specify the character delimiter." " " is default,"csv" format effective.
specify the field delimiter. "," is default,"csv" format effective.
specify the record delimiter."\n" is default,"csv" format effective.
specify the collectionspace name of exported data.
specify the collection name of exported data.
Specify import data field names."csv" format effective.
Specifies whether the first row of imported data as field names."false" is default,"csv" format effective.
Specify the import data, automatically add the field name."ture" is default,"csv" format effective.
Specify the import data, automatically add value."false" is default,"csv" format effective.

section.title: Usage
section.p: In the following sample,sdbimprt will import data into local database which port is 50000,and collectionspace name is foo,collection name is bar,and import type is csv,import file is test.csv.
section.codeblock: sdbimprt –h localhost –s 50000 -–type csv –-file test.csv -c foo -l bar

section.title: sdbload
section.p: sdbload is a batch import tool in SequoiaDB database, it can achieve from a JSON format data stored file import into Sequoiadb database.

The record of JSON format must meet the json definition:Left and right curly braces ({})as a record delimiter; and the string data type must be contained in doublequotation marks;and escape character is defined as the backslash(\).。

strow.stentry: return the basic help and usage information
import data from SequoiaDB of the specified hostname.In default,the sdbload try to connect to local hostname.
specify port.In default,the sdbload try to connect to the host which port number is 50000.
specify the character delimiter." " " is default,"csv" format effective.
specify the field delimiter. "," is default,"csv" format effective.
specify the record delimiter."\n" is default,"csv" format effective.
specify the collectionspace name of exported data.
specify the collection name of exported data.
specify the data capture mode, the default "io","io" or "hdfs" pattern can be.
Specified load library path, hafs mode is active.
Specifies the data source to connect the server's host name, hafs mode is active.
Specifies the data source to connect the service side of the port, hdfs mode is active.
specify the collection data source server user name, hdfs mode is active.
specify the import data file type, default "csv", "json" or "csv" type can be.
specify the import data filed names."csv" formats.
Specifies whether the first row of imported data as field names."false" is default,"csv" format effective.
Specify the import data, automatically add the field name."ture" is default,"csv" format effective.
Specify the import data, automatically add value."false" is default,"csv" format effective.

section.title: Usage
section.p: In the following sample,sdbload will import data into local database which port is 50000,and collectionspace name is foo,collection name is bar,and import file is test.json with asynchronous loading.
section.codeblock: sdbload –h localhost -s 50000 –-file test.json –c foo –l bar –type json
